source_file: 5420/10_18/ACLs and Classifier Policies Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about About applying ACLs to VLANs?

About applying ACLs to VLANs: ACLs can be applied to VLANs in the inbound (ingress) and outbound (egress) directions. Sequence numbering If no sequence number is specified, the software appends new ACEs to the end of the ACL with a sequence number equal to the highest ACE currently in the list plus 10. The sequence numbers may be resequenced using the access-list resequence command. ACL Logging On the 6200, 6300, and 6300L Switch Series: From 10.17 release onwards, ACL Logging will function properly on a VSF stack for packets received on a member that is not the conductor. The ACL name, sequence number, action, and other relevant details for the matching ACE will be logged. Public ACL usage tips 12 Deny ACLs If multiple ACLs of different types are applied in the same direction, a deny ACE, whether explicit or implicit, in one ACL overrides a permit ACL in another. A deny ACE is an ACE within an ACL that uses the deny action keyword.

section: VLAN ACLs and interaction with VXLAN traffic
source_file: 5420/10_18/ACLs and Classifier Policies Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about VLAN ACLs and interaction with VXLAN traffic?

VLAN ACLs and interaction with VXLAN traffic: NOTE This section applies to these AOS-CX platforms that support VXLAN and VXLAN ACLs: 5420, 6200, 6300, 6400, 8100, 8325, 8360, 9300/9300S, 10040, 10000. VLAN policies applied to an SVI will not match VXLAN traffic over a VNI. Interactions with VXLAN traffic on an L2 VNI About this task The way VLAN ACLs interact with VLAN traffic carried by VXLAN tunnels depends on the AOS-CX platform. For the purposes of this discussion, VLAN traffic is Ethernet traffic with an IP payload. VXLAN traffic includes a VXLAN header, encapsulating the Ethernet frame and IP header into the VXLAN UDP packet. VLAN traffic can be considered to be "normal" L2 traffic, and VXLAN traffic can be considered to be "tunneled" (encapsulated) L2 traffic.

topic: VLAN ACLs and interaction with VXLAN traffic

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the access-list secure-update command do?

The access-list secure-update command this command determines if access lists are updated using the secure-update feature. Secure-update is enabled by default. When secure update is enabled and an ACL is updated or replaced, one or more override entries are installed in the TCAM table(s) containing the ACL that is being modified. As a result, all traffic of the same type as the currently configured ACL will be denied on the interfaces to which the ACL is applied. This ensures that traffic is not temporarily allowed while modifying an ACL. Upon completion of the update, the TCAM override entries are uninstalled and traffic resumes ACL matching. The no version of this command disables this feature. If secure-update is disabled, there will be no override entry installed. Syntax: access-list secure-update.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Specifies the ACL type: ip for IPv4, IPv6 for IPv6, or mac for MAC ACL. Specifies the ACL name. Selects the inbound (ingress) traffic direction. Selects the outbound (egress) traffic direction. Public apply access-list (to interface or LAG) 77. Syntax: no apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in | out}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies the name of the policy. <POLICY-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies a sequence number for the policy entry. Optional. Ran ge: 1 to 4294967295. comment Stores the remaining entered text as a policy entry comment. class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS- NAME> Specifies a type of class, ip for IPv4, IPv6 for IPv6 and mac for a MAC policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what restrictions or notes are documented for ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations?

For ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: Switches have finite (TCAM and other) hardware resources used in the application of ACLs and Classifier policies (including Port-Access policies) to packets being processed in switch hardware. ADC (analytics data collection) also consumes TCAM lookups. Take the considerations described in this chapter into account when deciding what ACL and classifier policy-related features to use at the same time. Subtopics Public.

topic: ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about ACL and Policy hardware resource commands?

ACL and Policy hardware resource commands: Show Resources The show resources command allows users to see hardware resource consumption in the switch. These hardware resources include the following: • TCAM entries • High-Capacity TCAM entries On the 6200 and 6300 switch series, these resources are only reported per VSF member. The usage of high capacity TCAM can be viewed specifically using show system high-capacity-tcam. This command displays the feature that is currently configured, pending configuration, and the default feature present in high capacity TCAM.

topic: ACL and Policy hardware resource commands

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Ingress VLAN Policy?

Ingress VLAN Policy: • Ingress IPFIX • Long Prefix IPv6 Unicast Routes • Port Access Client • QoS WRED NOTE There is one dedicated Flex lookup used only by Group Based Policy (GBP) and Audio/Visual Bridging (AVB). This lookup uses entries from the same set of shared banks as other TCAM lookups. Due to both AVB and GBP both competing for this Flex lookup, only one of these features may be installed in a configuration. The following features compete with each other for egress lookups: • Egress Port IP ACL • Egress Port MAC ACL.

document_title: Acls And Classifier Policies Guide (5420, 6200 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_18/ACLs and Classifier Policies Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Port Access Client Policy Captive-portal?

Port Access Client Policy Captive-portal: L4 port ranges On platforms that support the use of dedicated resources called L4 Port Ranges, any ACE that uses lt, gt, range, or port groups attempts to use these dedicated hardware resources. L4 port ranges are used to reduce the number of TCAM entries needed to cover an L4 port range. For example, range 50000 5000 5 could be covered by a single L4 port range or by two TCAM entries using the following value/masks: Entry number L4 port range Value/Mask 1 2 range 50000 50003 0xC350/0xFFFC range 50004 50005 0xC354/0xFFFE Public L4 port ranges 214 The problem gets multiplied when an ACE matches on two L4 port ranges. The TCAM entries used to cover a source and destination L4 port range must cover all possibilities. For example, for this ACE, 10 permit tcp any range 100 200 any range 50000 50005 The range 100 200 needs six value masks. As a result, this ACE needs 12 (6*2=12) TCAM entries.

document_title: Acls And Classifier Policies Guide (5420, 6200 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show resources command do?

The show resources command shows hardware resource consumption for the specified VSF member or for all VSF members. Resource data is updated every 10 seconds. Hardware resource consumption information is shown for: • TCAM entries • TCAM lookups • Policers Public. Syntax: show resources [<SLOT-ID>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities command do?

The show capacities command shows system capacities and their values for all features or a specific feature. Specifies a feature. For example, aaa. Capacities are expressed in user-understandable terms. Thus they may not map to a specific hardware or software resource or component. They are not intended to define a feature exhaustively. Shows system capacities status and their values for all features or a specific feature. Specifies the feature, for example aaa for which to display capa cities, values, and status. Required. Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip proxy-arp command do?

The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP for the specified Layer 3 interface. Proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces, LAG interfaces, and VLAN interfaces. It is disabled by default. To enable proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables proxy ARP for the specified interface. Syntax: no ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor fall-over command do?

The neighbor fall-over command enables BGP fast peering session deactivation. When neighbor fall-over is configured, the BGP process monitors the RIB and if the route to peer is not present in the routing table, it immediately deactivates the peer session without waiting for the hold down timer. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables this feature. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> Enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. You can either configure BFD support for BGP per neighbor or peer-group. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE Multihop BFD is not supported for BGP. <IPv4-ADDR> Specifies the neighbor address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over.

Specifies the VTEP. <VNI-Value> Specifies the VNI. <ROUTE-TYPE-Value> Specifies the route type. Displays OSPF routing information. Syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir command do?

The ip pim-bidir command enables or disables PIM-Bidir in the designated interface. This command works in the interface context, including loopback. The IP address must be configured on the interface to enable BIDIR-PIM. Enables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Disables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Syntax: ip pim-bidir [enable | disable].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command changes the maximum time, in seconds, before the router transmits the initial PIM hello message on the current interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to default. The default value is 5. Configures the given value as the hello interval. Range: <0-5>. Public ip pim-bidir hello-delay 458. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command changes the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current subnet. The router uses hello packets to inform neighbor routers of its presence and to comput the hello hold time. Hello hold time informs neighbor routers estimated wait time for the next hello packet from the router. Shortening the hello interval reduces the hello hold time. Default is set to 30 seconds. The no form of this command resets the frequency of PIM hello messages back to the default value on the current subnet. Public ip pim-bidir hello-interval 460 Configures the given value as hello interval. Range: 1-300 seco nds. • The default value (30 seconds) covers most deployment needs; change only for explicit convergence or timing requirements. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. In chatty networks where there is a chance of Hello packets being lost, configuring a higher hold time reduces the likelihood of adjacency loss. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command do?

The ip pim-bidir override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command configures the override interval that gets inserted into the Override Interval field of a LAN prune delay option. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to the default. The default value is 2500 milliseconds. Public ip pim-bidir override-interval 465 <INTERVAL-VALUE> Specifies override interval value. Range: 500-6000. Default: 2500. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the diag cable-diagnostic command do?

The diag cable-diagnostic command provides information about the cable health after running a diagnostic test on an interface. If you run a new cable diagnostic command when a cable diagnostic is in progress for the interface, the new cable diagnostic command fails to execute. In such a scenario, an error message is displayed. On executing a cable diagnostic test command, it automatically clears the old test results before the new test starts. Specifies the name of the interface. <IF-NAME> Public Cable diagnostic commands 495 test <IF-NAME> Runs a cable diagnostic test on an interface. show <IF-NAME> clear <IF-NAME>. Syntax: diag cable-diagnostic.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command use this command to allow or block authentication on the CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit). This is allowed by default. The no form of this command prevents authentication on CDP packets received on the port. This command can be issued from the interface (config-if) or Link Aggregation Group (config-lag-if) contexts. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port or LAG. This command can be issued from the interface (config-if) or Link Aggregation Group (config-lag-if) contexts. The no form of this command blocks the CDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-proxy-logoff command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-proxy-logoff command allows a client to be logged off from the system via a special TLV in the CDP packet. By default, proxy logoff via CDP packet support is disabled. When allow-cdp-proxy-logoff is enabled, TLV received from CDP packets corresponding to logoff processing will be read and logoff is issued to the clients. This only works on client authentication enabled ports and aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu must be enabled to process. This command can be issued from the interface (config-if) or Link Aggregation Group (config-lag-if) contexts. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-proxy-logoff.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command configures a gateway address for the DHCP relay agent to use for DHCP requests. By default DHCP relay agent picks the lowest-numbered IP address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the gateway address. Specifies the IP address of the gateway in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a is a decimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: no ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command defines the address of a remote DHCP server or DHCP relay agent. Up to eight addresses can be defined. The DHCP relay agent forwards DHCP client requests to all defined servers. If IP helper adddress is defined with VRF argument then this command requires you define a source IP address for DHCP relay with the command ip source-interface. The configured source IP on the VRF is used to forward DHCP packets to the server. A helper address cannot be defined on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command removes an IP helper address. helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> Specifies the helper IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Public ip helper-address 842. Syntax: no ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping statistics command do?

The show dhcpv6-snooping statistics command shows the DHCPv6 snooping statistics. Specifies HTTP proxy location and VRF. When HTTP proxy location and VRF are configured on the switch, it overrides any existing HTTP proxy location and VRF as this has the highest priority over the values obtained from other sources. Following locations can be used for the HTTP proxy location: • A fully qualified domain name (FQDN). • An IPv4 address with colon separated port number Public DHCP options commands 962 • An IPv6 address with colon separated port number NOTE When configuring an IPv6 address with a port number, the address must be specified inside square brackets. An example - [2001: 0db8: 85a3: 0000: 0000: 8a2e: 0370: 7334]: 8080. If the command is entered without the VRF parameter, then the VRF used will be 'default' VRF. Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping statistics.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. Public ip dns domain-name 966 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command do?

The ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax:
- ip dns host 967
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

syntax:
- ip dns host 967
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <PORT0> <PORT1> Required, set port0 of the ring. Required, set port1 of the ring. Required, interface name (string). Public erps ring <RINGID> <port0|port1> interface 988. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> description command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <LINE> Guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Creates or enters the HPE Aruba Networking Central configuration context (config-hpe-anw-central). When a switch is able to connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the HPE Aruba Networking Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the HPE Aruba Networking Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to HPE Aruba Networking Central and will get disconnected again. Syntax: redundancy switchover.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip icmp unreachable command do?

The ip icmp unreachable command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages on the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The unreachable host address originates from the failed packed. Default setting. The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. This command does not prevent other hosts from sending an ICMP unreachable message. Syntax: no ip icmp unreachable.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Public ip igmp apply access-list 1195 Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. Syntax: no ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command do?

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Specifies an IGMP last-member-query-interval on the inter face. Default: 1 second. Range: 1-2 seconds. <INTERVAL-VALUE>. Syntax: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME> command do?

The ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME> command configures initial IGMP querier-wait-time value in seconds. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier-wait-time to the default value of 260 seconds. Note that the wait timer can be configured to any numbers within the 1-300 second range. Configures IGMP querier-wait-time to desired value. Public ip igmp querier-wait-time 1201 <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>. Syntax: no ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp static-group <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP> command do?

The ip igmp static-group <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP> command configures an IGMP static multicast group on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. You can configure a maximum of 32 IGMP static groups. The no form of the command unconfigures IGMP static multicast group on an interface. Specifies an IGMP static multicast group IP address on the inter face. Format: A. B. C. D <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP>. Syntax: no ip igmp static-group <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp version <VERSION> command do?

The ip igmp version <VERSION> command configures the IGMP version on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of the command configures the default IGMP version, 3, on the interface. Specifies the IGMP version on the interface. Select 2 for IGMPv 2 (RFC2236). Select 3 for IGMPv3 (RFC3376). Values: 2 or 3. <VERSION>. Syntax: no ip igmp version <VERSION>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp version <VERSION> strict command do?

The ip igmp version <VERSION> strict command configures an IGMP strict version on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Drops packets that do not match the configured version. The no form of the command removes the strict version configuration from the interface. Specifies the IGMP version on the interface. Select 2 for IGMPv 2 (RFC2236). Select 3 for IGMPv3 (RFC3376). Values: 2 or 3. Public ip igmp version strict 1209. Syntax: no ip igmp version <VERSION> strict.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp statistics command do?

The show ip igmp statistics command shows IGMP statistics, including groups joined, or shows statistics by VRF. Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs Configures drop-unknown and fastlearn modes on the ports. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. Fast learn enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [all-vrfs].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command do?

The ip igmp snooping all-vlans command enable IGMP snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables IGMP snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command is issued. When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, IGMP snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global IGMP snooping, allows IGMP snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level IGMP snooping settings. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface loopback <ID> command do?

The interface loopback <ID> command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Specifies the loopback interface ID. Range: 1 to 256 <INSTANCE> Public interface loopback 1270. Syntax: no interface loopback <ID>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN. Public interface vlan 1271 Specifies the loopback interface ID. <VLAN-ID> none. Syntax: no interface vlan <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip tcp mss <VALUE> command do?

The ip tcp mss <VALUE> command sets the TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) MSS (Maximum Segment Size) for an interface. When configured, the MSS option in the TCP header is set in the TCP handshake for negotiation during connection establishment. Once the TCP session is established, changing the TCP MSS value on the interface will not affect the existing session. The no form of this command removes the TCP MSS configuration from the interface. NOTE TCP MSS is supported on ROP, SVI, subinterface, and L3 GRE tunnel interface. Specifies the IPv4 TCP MSS in bytes. Range: 68 to 65495. <VALUE>. Syntax: no ip tcp mss <VALUE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 destination-guard command do?

The IPv6 destination-guard command enables IPv6 destination guard on a VLAN. Public IPv6 destination guard commands 1381 The no form of the command removes the IPv6 destination guard from a VLAN. NOTE To avoid dropping valid packets when destination guard is enabled, it is recommended to configure DHCPv6 snooping and ND snooping to populate the binding database. Syntax: no IPv6 destination-guard.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command do?

The ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command enables the IPv6-only-preferred mode on the VLAN interface, and is also supported on the OOBM interface. Once enabled, the DHCP client prioritizes a IPv6 address over an IPv4 address. To obtain a DHCPv4 address, the IPv6-only-preferred command must be disabled, and the DHCPv4 client must be restarted. This command is disabled by default. NOTE Ensure the server supports the IPv6-only-preferred mode option, as the command will not work unless supported by the server. The no form of this command disables IPv6-only-preferred mode. NOTE The IPv6-only-preferred mode command must be configured prior to the ip dhcp command, otherwise it will not take effect. Syntax: no ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command do?

The IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command sets a global unicast address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the global unicast address on the interface. NOTE This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-local command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link-local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command. Syntax: no IPv6 address <global-unicast-address>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. hop-limit <HOPLIMIT> Specifies the hop limit. Range: 0-255. Default: 64. Configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. Specifies the MTU size. Range: 1280-65535 bytes. Default: 1 500 bytes. <MTU-VALUE> Configures the ND time in milliseconds between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination. Increase the ns-interval time if the network is slow or if there are persistent retry failures. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives and update it to the default value. Specifies the lifetime of router advertisements sent from this in terface. Range: 4 to 9000 seconds. Syntax: no ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME> command specifies the maximum router advertisement interval. Public ip irdp maxadvertinterval 1434 The no form of this command removes the specified maximum router advertisement interval and reverts to the default value. Specifies the maximum time allowed between the sending of u nsolicited router advertisements. Range: 4 to 1800 seconds. De fault: 600 seconds. <TIME>. Syntax: no ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME> command specifies the minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements. By default, this value is automatically set by the switch to be 75% of the value configured for maximum router advertisement interval. Use this command to override the automatically configured value. The no form of this command removes the specified minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements and reverts to the default value. Specifies the minimum time allowed between the sending of u nsolicited router advertisements. Range: 3 to 1800 seconds. De fault: 450 seconds (75% of the default value for maximum route r advertisement interval). <TIME>. Syntax: no ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface persona lag <NAME> command do?

The interface persona lag <NAME> command creates a persona LAG, a template for LAG (Link Aggregation Group) interfaces. A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. The no form of this command deletes the persona LAG interface. Specifies the name of the persona LAG. <NAME>. Syntax: no interface persona lag <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary] command do?

The ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary] command sets an IPv4 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. One primary and up to 31 secondary address can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.1 00. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. Specifies a secondary IP address. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK> secondary. Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command do?

The aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command enables/disables privilege elevation for the users in an administrators group. When enabled, an administrative user is provided lower privilege access upon successful switch login. The user must execute an enable command and re-authenticate to elevate their privilege to administrator (the administrator level access with ‘#’ prompt). Executing the disable command revokes an administrator privilege and returns the user to the lower privilege access level (the operator level access ‘>’ prompt). The no form of this command disables privilege elevation for the administrator user group. Syntax: no aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication minimum-password-length <LENGTH> command do?

The aaa authentication minimum-password-length <LENGTH> command enables minimum password length checking. Existing passwords shorter than the minimum length are unaffected. Length checking does not apply to ciphertext passwords. Length checking applies both to local and remote authentication. The no form of this command disables minimum password length checking. Public aaa authentication minimum-password-length 1600 Specifies the minimum password length. Range: 1 to 32. <LENGTH>. Syntax: no aaa authentication minimum-password-length <LENGTH>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST> command do?

The aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST> command defines authorization as being basic local RBAC (specified as none), or as full-fledged local RBAC specified as local (the default), or as remote TACACS+ (specified with group <GROUP-LIST>). Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually. All server groups named in the command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. The no form of this command unconfigures authorization for the specified connection type, reverting to the default of local. NOTE Although only TACACS+ servers are supported for remote authorization, local authorization (basic or full-fledged) can be used with remote RADIUS authentication. Syntax: no aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show loop-protect command do?

The show loop-protect command this command shows the following global configurations. • Transmit interval. • Re-enable timer. • Per-port configurations. • Loop-protect enable or disable status. • Loop detection. • Loop detected count. • Timestamp of latest loop detection. • Loop is detected on VLAN. • Interface status. • List of configured VLAN's for that port. • VTEP port information Specify the interface name on display for the filter. When rebooting the switch or after switchover, The loop-detected count on the loop detected port is reset to zero. <INTERFACE-NAME> • An Ethernet interface associated with a physical port. Form Specifies the name of a logical interface on the switch. This can be one of the following: at: member/slot/port. • A LAG (link aggregation group). Specify the ID of LAG. Syntax: show loop-protect [<INTERFACE-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show mac-address-table static command do?

The show mac-address-table static command shows all statically configured MAC addresses. Shows MAC addresses learned by or configured on the specified VLAN. Specifies the VLAN ID. Displays the MAC entries in the MAC address table that have moved at least once. The output can be filtered based on a specific VLAN or specific MAC address and VLAN. Displays information for a specific MAC address. <address> <vlan> Adds a static MAC address to the MAC address table and associates it with a port or existing VLAN. Static MAC addresses can only be assigned to layer 2 (non-routed) interfaces. Static MAC addresses are not affected by the MAC address aging time. The no form of this command deletes a static MAC address. Specifies a MAC address (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. Syntax: show mac-address-table static.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command configures the drop unknown mode on the switch to the default vlan-shared. Required: Enable shared VLAN filter on the switch. vlan-exclusive Required: Enable exclusive drop unknown filter per VLAN. This command delays the refresh for some IGMP or MLD protocol-related values. When this command is enabled, IGMP/MLD last_reporter value, source, group, or querier uptime, and the create time and expiry time values will be updated based on the configured timer values. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the MLD join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Public IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list 1734 Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command enable MLD snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables MLD snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command is issued. When MLD snooping is enabled globally, MLD snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global MLD snooping, allows MLD snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level MLD snooping settings. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command this command configures the given ports in auto mode, which is the default port mode. The no form of this command disables auto ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as an auto port. Specifies the number of a single VL AN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by c ommas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 60). Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in blocked mode. The no form of this command removes blocked ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a blocked port. Specifies the number of a single V LAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 6 0). This command enables the port to learn group information on receiving topology change notification. The no form of this command disables fastlearn on the ports. Required: 1/1/1-1/1/2, ports to be configured as fastlearn por ts. Public IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn 1740. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn <port-list>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command configures the specified ports as fastleave ports. Enables the switch to immediately remove an interface from the bridge table upon receiving the leave group message. The no form of this command disables fastleave configuration on the ports. Public IPv6 mld snooping fastleave vlan 1741 Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a fastleave port. Specifies the number of a single VLAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 60). MLD fastleave is configured for ports on a per-VLAN basis. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>].

Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a forced fastleave port. Specifies the number of a single VLAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, sepa rated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (1 0-40, 60). messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in forward mode. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a forward port. Specifies the number of a single V LAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 6 0). This command configures the preprogramming of the starg (*, G) flow feature on MLD snooping enabled VLANs. Enable preprogramming (*, G) flows on the VLAN. Disable prprogramming (*, G) flows on the VLAN. When this feature is enabled, a summarized multicast bridge entry is programmed into the hardware table when a (*, G) or sg MLD join is received on the MLD snooping enabled VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping preprogram-starg-flow {enable | disable}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of MLD reports over the fabric. By default, when MLD snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging MLD reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 mld command do?

The show IPv6 mld command this command shows MLD groups joined details. Public MLD show commands for interface VLAN 1774 Show MLD snooping info for all VRFs in all interfaces or groups, or for all VRFs in a specified group, interface or VLANshow all MLD counters, or display counters for the specified in terface or VLAN group <x: x: : x: x> [source <x: x: : x: x>] Show MLD group information for the specified group, group an d interface, or group and vlan. Include the optional source <x: x: : x: x> parameter to dislay source information for the group. groups interface Show MLD group information for all VRFs, or for groups in the s pecified interface or VLAN. Shows MLD configuration information for a specified interface o r VLAN. Syntax: show IPv6 mld.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command do?

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> Displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> Public show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 1810 Shows MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Public show spanning-tree mst interface 1812 Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> <IFNAME> Shows spanning tree port summary information. Shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID> command do?

The ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID> command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Specifies the identification number of the key to set as trusted. Range: 1 to 65534. <KEY-ID>. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Public router ospf 2032 Specifies an OSPF process ID. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name for the OSPF process. Default: default. Syntax: no router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router-id (ospf) command do?

The router-id (ospf) command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Specifies the Router ID in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. <ROUTER-ADDR> Sets the SHA (secure hash authentication) key for the selected virtual link. If the SHA key is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol Specifies the area ID is one of the following formats. • Area ID in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255. • Area ID as a decimal value. Range: 0-4294967295. Specifies the OSPFv3 process ID. Range: 1 to 65535. <AREA-ID> <PROCESS-ID> Public IPv6 ospfv3 area 2094 Configures IPSec AH authentication. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> <ENCR-TYPE> Specifies the Security Parameters Index (SPI) to use. The SPI is an identification tag carried in the IPsec AH header. It enables t he receiving OSPF process to select and use the Security Association (SA) from the SA table. The SPI must be unique on the sw itch. Range: 256 to 4294967295. Specifies the authentication type: md5 or sha1. Specifies the key type to use: plaintext (unencrypted), hex-st ring (encrypted) or ciphertext (encrypted). Specifies the authentication key. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-dense hello-holdtime command do?

The ip pim-dense hello-holdtime command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. If not configured, the implementation derives the value from the current hello-interval. Syntax: ip pim-dense hello-holdtime <holdtime-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip mroute (PIM-DM) command do?

The show ip mroute (PIM-DM) command shows multicast routing information. Optionally, you can show specific information by VRF. If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. Shows mroute information for all VRFs. Optional. Shows mroute information for a particular VRF. If the <VRF-N AME> is not specified, it shows information for the default VRF. Optional. Shows the multicast routing information for the given group address. Optionally, you can specify display information by VRF. If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. <GROUP-ADDR> <SOURCE-ADDR> Specifies a group address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies show information for the group from this source in IP v 4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: show ip mroute [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

Shows the PIM router information. Optionally, you can specify display information by VRF. If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. Optional. Shows PIM router information on all VRFs. Optional. Shows PIM router information for a particular VRF. If t he <VRF-NAME> is not specified, it shows information for the default VRF. Shows the information about PIM interfaces currently configured in the router. Optionally, you can specify display information by VRF. Syntax: show ip mroute brief [al-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command do?

The ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command enables the router to accept multicast traffic from remote sources. When enabled, the router accepts multicast traffic from sources that are not directly connected and acts as the First Hop Router (FHR), performing registration to the Rendezvous Point (RP). In scenarios where a Gateway BD is configured in EVPN OISM networks, this should be applied to the external router interface that connects to the Gateway BD. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. Syntax: no ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-sparse lan-prune-delay command do?

The IPv6 pim6-sparse lan-prune-delay command enables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. The default is enabled. With LAN-prune-delay enabled, the router informs downstream neighbors how long it will wait before pruning a flow after receiving a prune request. Other downstream routers on the same interface must send a join to override the prune before the LAN-prune-delay time to continue the flow. Prompts any downstream neighbors with multicast receivers continuing to belong to the flow to reply with a join. If no joins are received after the LAN-prune-delay period, the router prunes the flow. The propagation-delay and override-interval settings determine the LAN-prune-delay setting. Public IPv6 pim6-sparse lan-prune-delay 2566 The no form of this command disables the LAN prune delay option. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-sparse lan-prune-delay.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-sparse override-interval command do?

The IPv6 pim6-sparse override-interval command configures the override interval that gets inserted into the Override Interval field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets the value to the default of 2500 ms. Specifies the override interval of a LAN Prune Delay option in m s. Range: 500 to 6000. Default: 2500. <INTERVAL-VALUE> Configures the propagation delay that gets inserted into the LAN prune delay field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 500 ms. Specifies the propagation delay value in ms. Range: 250 to 200 0. Default: 500. Public IPv6 pim6-sparse propagation-delay 2569 Configures the frequency at which the router will send periodic join or prune-interval messages. Syntax: IPv6 pim6-sparse override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: no crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Public crypto pki certificate 2662 Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command configures the authentication mechanism used to control access to the network. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 5420 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period command configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Configures the period the port waits to retransmit the next EAPOL request identity frame on an 802.1X enabled port that has no authenticated clients. The no form of the command resets the discovery period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the discovery period (in seconds). Default: 30. Range: 1 to 65535. <PERIOD> Configure the maximum size in bytes of an EAP-TLS fragment encoded in a single RADIUS request packet. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator discovery-period <PERIOD>.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator discovery-period <PERIOD>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command configures the switch to use an existing RADIUS server group for 802.1X authentication globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the server group to the default, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 2753 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command enables periodic reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic reauthentication. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 2755 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... Configures the period after which the authenticated clients are reauthenticated on the port. You must enable reauthentication on the port before configuring the reauthentication period. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-rea... 2869 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h c a c h e d - r e a... Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius ser... 2873 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r a d i u s s e r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command do?

The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>} command do?

The qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>} command configures a system-wide mapping from internal local priority to an 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP value. The qos egress-rewrite cos command allows the PCP value to be rewritten on egress, and the qos egress-rewrite dscp command allows the DSCP value to be rewritten on egress. The local priority associated with a packet affects only its behavior within the switch. By default, it does not influence the priority value written to the packet upon transmission. This command, along with its no form, accepts a single local priority, a range of local priorities, or a comma-separated list of local priorities and ranges. This provides a convenient method for configuring multiple entries in the map. Syntax: no qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory-default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos schedule-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos schedule-profile <NAME> command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: no qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos shape command do?

The qos shape command limits the egress bandwidth on an interface to a value that is lower than its line rate. Errors will be generated in the following events: • A user configures a port-shaping value that is greater than 100 percent • A user configures a kbps port-shaping value that is less than the supported minimum kbps value. The supported minimum kbps shaping value can be retrieved using the show capacities command. The no form of this command removes shaping from an interface. Specifies the maximum traffic rate in kbps. Range: 1 to 10000 0000. Alternatively, the bandwidth can also be configured as a percentage of link bandwidth. The supported range is 1-100. Default units are kilobits per second. Syntax: qos shape <RATE> [kbps|percent].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no aaa accounting port-access command do?

The no aaa accounting port-access command configures port access accounting information that is captured for 802.1X and MAC-authenticated clients. Defines port access accounting as being local (with the parameter local) (the default). Or defines port access accounting as being remote (with the parameter group <GROUP-NAME>) with a sequence of remote RADIUS servers in a single RADIUS server group to be accessed for port access accounting purposes. For remote RADIUS port access accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote RADIUS server in the specified group. If a user-defined RADIUS server group is named in your command, it must exist. The no form of this command works as follows: • no aaa accounting port-access: Globally unconfigures port access accounting. Syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim.

syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command do?

The radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before trying the next RADIUS server. If a value is not specified, a default value of 5 seconds is used. You can override this value with a fine-grained per server timeout configured for individual servers. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global authentication timeout to the default of 5 seconds. Specifies the timeout interval of 1 to 60 seconds. Default: 5 sec onds. Syntax: no radius-server timeout [<1-60>].

If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the tacacs-server key command do?

The tacacs-server key command creates or modifies a TACACS+ global passkey. The TACACS+ global passkey is used as a shared-secret for encrypting the communication between all TACACS+ servers and the switch. The TACACS+ global passkey is required for authentication unless local passkeys have been set. By default, the TACACS+ global passkey is empty. If the administrator has not set this key, the switch will not be able to perform TACACS+ authentication. The switch will instead rely on the authentication mechanism configured with aaa authentication login. NOTE When this command is entered without parameters, plaintext passkey prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered passkey characters are masked with asterisks. The no form of the command removes the global passkey. Syntax: tacacs-server key [plaintext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY> | ciphertext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies VRF name. Syntax: no router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear ip rip statistics command do?

Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. Public RIP v2 clear commands 3242 Enables RIP process for RIP enabled IP address configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on interface. Disables RIP process for RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process on interface. Disables an interface from sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. The no form of this command enables interface for sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. Disables interface from receiving RIP packets for a specific IP address. Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports (RPVST) command do?

The show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports (RPVST) command shows ports blocked by STP protection functions such as Root guard, Loop guard, BPDU guard, and RPVST guard. Specifies a VLAN ID number. <VLAN-ID> Shows a summary of port-related spanning-tree configuration and status. Shows the summary of spanning tree root and configurations for all VLANs. Displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN and optionally displays details on the RPVST instance for the VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. Public show spanning-tree vlan detail 3311 Enables and configures the auto re-enable timeout in seconds for all interfaces with BPDU guard enabled. Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [vlan <VLAN-ID>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities smartlink command do?

The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Enables the notification of events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. It is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the event traps. Enables sending SNMP traps for LLDP related events from a particular interface. LLDP trap generation is enabled by default on all the interfaces and has to be disabled for interfaces on which traps are not required to be generated. The no form of this command disables the LLDP trap generation. NOTE LLDP trap generation is disabled by default at the global level and must be enabled before any LLDP traps are sent. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command associates an SNMP MIB view with the SNMP community. The no form of this command removes the associated SNMP MIB view from the SNMP community. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. <STRING> <VIEW-NAME>. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.
