source_file: 5420/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER> command do?

The map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER> command assigns a local priority to a queue in a queue profile. By default, the larger the queue number the higher its priority. A queue without a local priority value assigned to it is not used to store packets. The same queue can be assigned multiple local priorities. The no form of this command removes the specified local priority from a specific queue. If no local priority number is specified, then all local priorities are removed from the queue. Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. <QUEUE-NUMBER> <PRIORITY-NUMBER>. Syntax: map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the NOTE command do?

The NOTE command assigns the Guaranteed Minimum Bandwidth (GMB) algorithm and a percentage of bandwidth to a queue. GMB allocates available bandwidth among all non-empty queues in relation to their configured minimum bandwidth. Non-empty queues are serviced first in strict order up to their minimum bandwidth. If there is any remaining bandwidth, the scheduler will strictly service any remaining non-empty queues. Egress queue shaping can be configured using the max-bandwidth option to limit the amount of traffic transmitted per output queue at all times, even when there is leftover bandwidth available on the port. The buffer associated with each egress queue stores the excess traffic to smooth the output rate. Syntax: NOTE.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> command do?

The name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> command assigns a description to a queue in a queue profile. This is for identification purposes and has no effect on configuration. The no form of this command removes the description associated with a queue. Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. <QUEUE-NUMBER> <DESCRIPTION> Specifies a queue description for identification purposes. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), undersco re (_), and hyphen (-). Examples Assigning the description priority-traffic to queue 7: switch(config)# qos queue-profile myprofile switch(config-queue)# name queue 7 priority-traffic Removing the description from queue 7: switch(config)# qos queue-profile myprofile switch(config-queue)# no name queue 7. Syntax: name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos cos command do?

The qos cos command configures a CoS PCP remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. Packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured CoS PCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If QoS trust mode is not set to none, then the remark is ignored, and the following commands will show the CoS remark status as ignored (incompatible Port Access Trust configuration) or not applied' (incompatible QoS global/port Trust configuration): • show running-configuration • show interface <PORT-NUM> • show interface <PORT-NUM> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Specifies an 802.1 VLAN priority CoS value. Range: 0 to 7. <CODE-POINT>. Syntax: qos cos <CODE-POINT>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite command do?

The qos egress-rewrite command enables a rewrite of the 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values for all traffic egressing on the configured interface. The PCP or DHCP value is determined by a globally configured egress priority rewrite map based on the traffic's local priority. Use this command to configure a system-wide mapping from internal local priorities to 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values. Public qos egress-rewrite 59 The no form of this command disables egress priority rewrite on the interface. Use show qos egress-rewrite-map to view the current global egress priority rewrite map. Use show qos egress-rewrite-map default to view the factory default egress priority rewrite map. Syntax: qos egress-rewrite [cos|dscp].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory-default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos schedule-profile command do?

The qos schedule-profile command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Public qos schedule-profile 64 Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos tunnel command do?

The qos tunnel command this command is used to configure the DSCP value populated in the tunnel IP header for non-IP packets. This is necessary to ensure that proper priority treatment can be achieved for the non-IP packets as they pass through the tunnel. If left unconfigured, the default mapping uses a DSCP value of 0 in the tunnel encapsulation header for all PCP values. Specifies the Priority Code Point (PCP) value to be mapped. Th e PCP value ranges from 0 to 7. Specifies the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value t hat needs to be populated on the tunnel header for non ip traffi c. The DSCP value ranges from 0 to 63. Default: 0 <CODE-POINT> <DSCP>. Syntax: qos tunnel cos <CODE-POINT> dscp-map <DSCP>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}} command do?

The rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}} command sets the amount of traffic of a specific type that can ingress on an Ethernet interface, or on each port of a LAG interface. Rate limits are enforced separately on each individual member of a LAG, not on the LAG as a whole. The no form of this command removes the traffic limit for the specified traffic type. Public rate-limit 77 {broadcast|multicast| unknown-unicast|icmp {ip- all|ip|IPv6} <RATE> {kbps|percent|pps} Specifies the type of ingress traffic to which the rate limit appli es: broadcast, multicast, unknown-unicast, or ICMP. The multicast rate limit affects multicast and broadcast traffic. The broadcast rate limit only affects broadcast traffic. When bot h types are applied to the same interface, broadcast packets a re limited to the lower of the two rate values. Syntax: rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface queue-monitor command do?

The show interface queue-monitor command display queue statistics history in the CLI as either a list or a histogram, or as a table, showing the previous five minutes of queue statistics history. interface [<IFNAME>] Specifies the name of an Ethernet port or LAG on the switch. If the optional <IFNAME> parameter is omitted, the output of this command displays all queue statistic data for all interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> or lag <ID> histogram|list|table Specifies the output format for queue monitor output. • histogram - Queue statistics history is presented in histog ram format for the last 8 hours. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>} queue-monitor.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command do?

The clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command clears the 802.1X authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator clients attached to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all 802.1X enabled ports. Specifies the interface name. <IF-NAME>. Syntax: clear dot1x authenticator statistics [interface <IF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-metho... 848 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h a u t h - m e t h o. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-rea... 849 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h c a c h e d - r e a... The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius ser... 854 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r a d i u s s e r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command do?

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command configures the data client limit on the multidomain enabled interface. By default, the data client limit on a multidomain enabled interface is 1, and the maximum number of data clients supported on a multidomain enabled port is 5. Specifies the maximum data client limit on the multidomain ena bled interface. Range: 1 to 5. <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show port-access clients command do?

The show port-access clients command shows summarized active port access client information. NOTE The User-Role column in the output will not display any value for clients not using local, downloaded or RADIUS overridden role. When an explicit client name is not available, only the MAC address of the client will be displayed. The VLANs in the output display the tags, u, t, and multi to indicate untagged VLAN, single tagged VLAN, and multiple VLANs respectively. Shows DHCP information of port access clients. dhcp NOTE To view the DHCP information of port access cl ients, either client IP tracker or DHCP snooping must be enabled. If client IP tracker is enabled, then the command does not display the lease ti me. This command does not display information about tagged VLAN. Shows port access information about UBT clients. Syntax: show port-access clients [dhcp-info|ubt|vxlan] [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show port-access clients traffic-inspection command do?

The show port-access clients traffic-inspection command this command displays information related to traffic inspection. In the detailed view, inspection data for the clients currently onboarded in the system is displayed. In the summary view, inspection data per signature is summarized based on the system uptime. Public show port-access clients traffic-inspection 914 Show traffic inspection information. traffic-inspection interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the interface name. mac <MAC-ADDRESS> Specifies the client MAC address. method {<device-profile>| <dot1x>|<mac-auth>} Displays the information for a specific onboarded-method. Th e method can be: • device-profile: Display clients on-boarded by the device -profile method. • dot1x: Display clients on-boarded by the dot1x method. • mac-auth: Display clients on-boarded by the mac-auth method. Syntax: show port-access clients traffic-inspection [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show port-access port-security violation clie command do?

The show port-access port-security violation clie command shows information on the number of client-limit-exceeded security violations that have occurred. The output can be filtered by interface. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name or a comma-separated list of inte rfaces, or a hyphen-separated interface range. Syntax: show port-access port-security violation client-limit-exceeded interface.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command do?

The clear dot1x supplicant statistics command clears the 802.1X supplicant statistics associated with the interface. If no interface is specified, the statistics are cleared for all 802.1X supplicant-enabled interfaces. Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant statistics are cleared. <IFRANGE> Configures the time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator on the other end before considering the link to be non-802.1X-capable and opening the interface on the data-plane. On a timeout, the switch will not use the authentication result to determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap. If not set, the switch will wait for the 802.1X authentication cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the interface. Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa radius-attribute group command do?

The aaa radius-attribute group command configures an existing RADIUS server group for which the configured RADIUS attributes will be included in request packets. Enters the config-radius-attr context. Public Configurable RADIUS attribute commands 1076 The no form of this command unconfigures the RADIUS server group for the configured RADIUS attributes. NOTE Nas-id and tunnel-private-group-id attributes only apply to port access requests. Nas-ip-addr attributes only apply to management user requests. Specifies an existing RADIUS server group name. <GROUP-NAME> For the selected (by context) RADIUS server group, configures the Network Access Server (NAS) ID request type for which the attribute configured with command nas-id value will be included. The no form of this command unconfigures the specified request type. Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-proxy enable command do?

The radius-proxy enable command enables RADIUS proxy at the global level. RADIUS proxy is disabled by default. This command is required for the RADIUS proxy functionality to work on the switch. The no form of this command disables the RADIUS proxy functionality. Syntax: radius-proxy enable.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-proxy profile command do?

The radius-proxy profile command configures the RADIUS proxy profile. The profile has all of the client and the server details to facilitate the proxy functionality. The profule must be enabled at the current context level after configuring all of the required parameters along with enabling the RADIUS proxy globally. A maximum of 32 RADIUS profiles can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the RADIUS proxy profile. Selects the name of the RADIUS proxy profile. Maximum of 32 c haracters. Public radius-proxy profile 1133. Syntax: radius-proxy profile <PROFILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-ip command do?

The class gbp-ip command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv4 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv4 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Public class gbp-ip 1217 Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv4 packets. {match | ignore} <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> <MAX-PORT> <ICMP-TYPE-VALUE> <ICMP-CODE-VALUE> <app-category-name> Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Syntax: [no] class gbp-ip <CLASS-NAME>.

source_file: 5420/10_18/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

Syntax: [no] class gbp-ip <CLASS-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-IPv6 command do?

The class gbp-IPv6 command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv6 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets.. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv6 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv6 packets. Public class gbp-IPv6 1224 <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> <MAX-PORT> <ICMP-TYPE-VALUE> <ICMP-CODE-VALUE> <app-category-name> Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Range: 0 to 65535. Syntax: class gbp-IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-mac command do?

The class gbp-mac command creates, deletes, or modifies class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on MAC information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> {match | ignore} Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified packets. Specifies the source role name. Public class gbp-mac 1230 Specifies the destination role name. <DST-ROLE-NAME> <NUMERIC-ETHERTYPE> Specifies the Ether Type number. Range: 0x600 to 0xffff. Syntax: class gbp-mac <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear port-access gbp hitcounts command do?

The clear port-access gbp hitcounts command clears the statistics of the group based policy applied on the client. Specifies the CDP TLV number. Supported values are 1 to 6, 10, and 11. <TLV-NUMBER>. Syntax: clear port-access gbp [<POLICY-NAME>] hitcounts {client}.
